responseMessageContents.text: I've got the RENDER_API_KEY as a private environment variable now, so I need to validate it against the Render API and figure out how to pass it to the bridge worker in the functions directory. The key question is whether the Cloudflare worker will have access to this private env var, so let me start by testing the key to confirm it works.

The environment variables are set up according to the message, but the sandbox process isn't seeing them in process.env — they're injected at startup or might need the shell to re-read them. Since private env vars are available in the Cloudflare worker environment rather than exposed directly to the sandbox shell, I should verify this by checking the worker env through the env-check action.

The Render API key is now set up in the Worker environment and looks valid. I need to run the render-test through the bridge to confirm it's working, then attempt the full end-to-end deployment. The GitHub token is still returning a 401 error, which was blocking code pushes, so I should verify the github-test again through the bridge to see if the user updated that token as well, even though they were only asked for the Render key.

The core issue is that without a valid GitHub token, I can't push the commit with the fixes to the repository. Since Render deploys from the GitHub main branch, deploying without pushing the updated code would just redeploy the old version. The bridge worker's github-push-files action also relies on the same revoked token, so that won't help either. Let me run both tests now to see the current state.

input.command: curl -s --max-time 30 -X POST "https://ivx26-backend.rork.app/" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"action":"render-test"}'; echo; curl -s --max-time 30 -X POST "https://ivx26-backend.rork.app/" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"action":"github-test"}' | sed 's/gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9_]*/[REDACTED]/g' | head -c 700

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"command": "curl -s --max-time 30 -X POST \"https://ivx26-backend.rork.app/\" -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -d '{\"action\":\"render-test\"}'; echo; curl -s --max-time 30 -X POST \"https://ivx26-backend.rork.app/\" -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -d '{\"action\":\"github-test\"}' | sed 's/gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9_]*/[REDACTED]/g' | head -c 700"}

responseMessageContents.text:
The Render key is working, but the GitHub token is still revoked, which blocks pushing the new commit. Even though the repo is public, I can't push without authentication. I'm considering whether Render supports alternative deployment methods beyond GitHub pushes.

Looking at the environment variables more carefully — the private env vars include GITHUB_TOKEN and GITHUB_REPO, and they're sent to the worker, not the sandbox. The worker's GITHUB_TOKEN might have been updated when the Render key was provided, so I should verify what the current token status actually is before requesting new credentials again.Render access is restored and verified, but the GitHub token is still revoked and blocking the pipeline at push—I need you to generate a new GitHub token with `repo` scope at github.com → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens, then paste it here so we can push your 45 tasks and 9 business fixes live.

envs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
envs.description: New GitHub personal access token with repo scope for ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest. The stored token was revoked by GitHub (verified 401 from production). Create at github.com → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Generate new token (classic), scope: repo.

input.reason: GitHub returns 401 Bad credentials for the stored token from the production worker itself \u2014 the token was revoked at GitHub's side. Without a valid token, commit 70e0af2 (all pending fixes) cannot be pushed, so a deploy would only re-ship the old code.
